fields.content: ⚠ Integrative Oncology Principle: Ayurveda in cancer is SUPPORTIVE/ADJUVANT — reduces treatment toxicity, improves quality of life, enhances immunity. NEVER advise stopping conventional oncology treatment. NEVER claim Ayurveda cures cancer. Always coordinate with the treating oncologist.

🌿 Karkatarbhuda Chikitsa Sutra: (1) Oja Raksha — protect vital essence. (2) Deepana-Pachana — clear Ama if ECOG 0–1. (3) Rasayana — Ashwagandha, Guduchi, Amalaki to restore Dhatu after chemo/radiation. (4) Sattvavajaya — psychological support essential. (5) Shamana only — Shodhana contraindicated in Asadhya/debilitated stage.

fields.label: ECOG Performance Status
options: ECOG 1 — Restricted in strenuous activity; ambulatory; light work
ECOG 2 — Ambulatory >50% of waking hrs; capable of self-care; no work
ECOG 3 — Limited self-care; confined to bed/chair >50% waking hrs
ECOG 4 — Completely disabled; no self-care; totally confined to bed/chair

fields.label: Karnofsky Score (%)
options: 70 — Cares for self; unable to do normal work

fields.label: Arbuda Classification
options: Vataja Arbuda (dry, hard, immobile, less pain)
Mansaja Arbuda (fleshy, solid, large — sarcoma equivalent)

fields.label: Integrative Treatment Notes
fields.placeholder: Rasayana plan (Ashwagandha / Guduchi / Amalaki), Sattvavajaya counselling, contraindications for Shodhana, pain management (Agni Karma / Lepas), coordination with oncologist…

fields.label: Ayurveda–Drug Interaction Note
fields.placeholder: Herbs to avoid during chemo (e.g. St John's wort, high-dose antioxidants during radiation), immunostimulants contraindicated during immunosuppression…

steps.title: General Inspection — Cancer Signs
items: Cachexia — temporal wasting, muscle loss, sunken eyes
Pallor (anaemia — chemo / disease), jaundice (hepatic / haemolytic)
Lymphoedema — arm (breast CA post-axillary dissection), leg (cervical CA / inguinal nodes)
Skin — radiation fibrosis, chemotherapy pigmentation, radiation dermatitis, alopecia
Venous access devices — port-a-cath, PICC line (note site + condition)

steps.title: Lymph Node Survey (mandatory)
items: Cervical — anterior / posterior / submandibular / supraclavicular (Virchow's node = gastric / lung)
Axillary — levels I/II/III (breast CA sentinel nodes)
Inguinal — inguinal + femoral chains (lower limb / genital / rectal)
Characteristics — size, consistency (hard = malignant, soft = reactive), mobility, matting, tenderness
Note ALL palpable nodes — any node >1cm in cancer patient is suspicious

steps.title: Primary Site Examination
items: Breast — quadrant, size (cm), consistency, fixity to skin/muscle, nipple discharge, peau d'orange
Oral cavity — floor of mouth, tongue, buccal mucosa, palate; measure lesion in 2 dimensions
Abdomen — liver size (Yakrut Pramana), ascites (shifting dullness, fluid thrill), masses
Rectal — digital examination for rectal / prostate CA
Skin lesion — ABCDE rule; measure, photograph if possible

steps.title: Ayurveda — Bala & Ojakshaya Assessment
items: Nadi — assess for Oja kshaya (thin, irregular, depleted quality)
Sparsha — skin temperature, turgor, muscle mass (Mamsa/Meda kshaya)
Dhruk — colour, icterus, pallor (Rakta kshaya)
Akriti — cachexia grade (mild/moderate/severe), body composition loss
Satwa — mental resilience (important for Sattvavajaya therapy planning)

steps.title: Treatment Toxicity Assessment (if on chemotherapy)
items: Peripheral neuropathy — glove-stocking sensory loss (taxanes, platinum)
Mucositis — oral mucosa grade (WHO 0–4)
Hand-foot syndrome — palmar/plantar erythema, blisters
Bowel — diarrhoea (irinotecan), constipation (vinca alkaloids / opioids)
Cardiac — ankle oedema, JVP (cardiotoxic agents: anthracyclines, Herceptin)
